office_does: One of 49 Washington State Senators, elected to a four-year term to write and vote on state law and budgets covering tax, housing, healthcare, education, and transportation policy.
race_blurb: Only one candidate filed for this South King County seat around Sea-Tac Airport: appointed incumbent Tina L. Orwall (D), who advances from the primary unopposed. She was appointed in December 2024 to succeed longtime Senator Karen Keiser after 15+ years in the State House.

candidates.summary: Appointed incumbent State Senator and career social worker, serving the 33rd District in the Legislature since 2009, first in the House (including as Speaker Pro Tempore) and in the Senate since December 2024.
highlights: Appointed to the Senate in December 2024 after roughly 15 years as a 33rd District state representative, including service as Speaker Pro Tempore [S1][S3]

Career social worker with twenty years in behavioral health; Vice Chair of the Senate Health & Long-Term Care Committee, with priorities including the 988 crisis line and behavioral-health capacity [S1]
Cites bills prohibiting insurers from using AI to deny care and a 2024 law against non-consensual deepfake intimate images, plus airport pollution mitigation for Sea-Tac communities [S1][S2]

spending.basis: Platform centers on investing in behavioral-health capacity, flood recovery, and backstopping programs threatened by federal cuts.

safety.basis: Behavioral-health career and priorities (988 crisis line, behavioral-health capacity, protecting vulnerable populations) emphasize prevention and services.

experience.basis: In the Legislature since 2009 with House leadership (Speaker Pro Tempore) and Senate committee vice-chairmanship — a long enacted record.

healthcare.basis: Healthcare work operates within the existing system — insurer regulation (AI denials), 988 crisis line, and behavioral-health capacity — with no single-payer platform.

tech.basis: Enacted record of AI and tech regulation: prohibiting insurers from using AI to deny care, protections against 'unregulated AI,' and the 2024 deepfake intimate-images law.
